data.title: Costamare announces plans for Costamare Bulkers spin-off
data.description:
• Costamare (NYSE:CMRE) said on Thursday that its board has approved the previously announced spin-off of its dry bulk business into a standalone company, Costamare Bulkers. The company expects to complete the transaction, by way of a pro rata distribution to Costamare shareholders, on May 6, 2025.
  • To accomplish the spin-off, Costamare will distribute all the Costamare Bulkers shares held by Costamare to holders of Costamare common shares on a pro rata basis. 
  • Holders of Costamare common shares will receive one common share of Costamare Bulkers for every five Costamare common shares held as of the record date. Costamare shareholders will receive cash upon the sale of the aggregated fractional shares in lieu of any fractional shares.
  • On May 7, 2025, Costamare Bulkers shares are expected to be listed and traded “regular-way” on the NYSE under the ticker symbol “CMDB.” Costamare will continue to trade on the NYSE under the ticker symbol “CMRE.”
  • Costamare (NYSE:CMRE) shares were up more than 7% in pre-market trading.

data.title: Apple keeps Outperform rating at Evercore amid US-China trade tussle
data.description:
Evercore maintained its Outperform rating and $250 price target on Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) while highlighting the iPhone maker's supply chain and diversity amid a growing trade battle between the U.S. and China.

Analysts led by Amit Daryanani said that Apple’s efforts to diversify its supply chain should help minimize the impact from tariffs that only target China. "If tariffs are applied broadly, we think Applewould continue to import and push through relevant price increase to offset the cost inflation along with getting suppliers to share the burden," the analysts added.

The analysts noted that Apple is at the center of ongoing trade tensions between the U.S. and China. They added that while the company did secure an exemption to the initial over 100% reciprocal tariffs (China), the U.S. administration has indicated that electronics will be included in the Section 232 investigation (Sector Tariff).

Daryanani and his team said Apple’s supply chain remains heavily China-centric, but the company has made efforts to diversify since the “trade war” during Trump’s first term. Diversification efforts have largely consisted of shifting some iPhone production to India and Wearables/Mac production to Vietnam.

Apple generates 37% of sales in the U.S. ( around 85M iPhone units), the analysts estimate the company can meet 35% of iPhone and iPad demand from India and the remaining from China. 

For wearables and Macs, the analysts forecast that half of that can be comfortably met from Vietnam, while the remainder will come from China.

The analysts noted that there are several offsets, including — sharing the cost with suppliers; moving more to India; price increases; and sustaining exemptions for longer ($500B U.S. investment/4 years).

Daryanani and his team added that even if tariffs extend beyond China, they do not think Apple will produce the iPhone in the U.S. because the higher labor costs alone would drive a more than 50% increase in iPhone manufacturing costs. This implies that even under a 50% global baseline tariff, it would not make financial sense to produce the iPhone domestically, the analysts added.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Kratos receives $30 million sole source air defense system hardware production award
data.description:
• Kratos Defense & Security Solutions (NASDAQ:KTOS) announced on Thursday that it has received an approximate $30 million sole source air defense system hardware production award. 
  • Work under this production award will be performed at a secure Kratos manufacturing facility, said the company.
  • Shares of KTOS were up +0.69% to $33.5 in premarket trading.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: ECB slashes rates by 25bps amid tariff uncertainty
data.description:
The European Central Bank (ECB) cut its interest rate by 25 basis points to 2.25% on Thursday, amid market volatility triggered by U.S. President Donald Trump’s trade policies.

The interest rates on the deposit facility, the main refinancing operations, and the marginal lending facility will be decreased to 2.25%, 2.40%, and 2.65%, respectively, with effect from 23 April.

The cut made it the seventh reduction since it began easing monetary policy in June 2024.

"The euro area economy has been building up some resilience against global shocks, but the outlook for growth has deteriorated owing to rising trade tensions," the statement said.

With the euro’s recent appreciation against the dollar and decreasing inflation in the Euro Area, market projections suggest the ECB may also implement a rate cut during its June meeting, as global trade and market turmoil induced by President Trump’s tariff announcement brought back growth concerns for the Eurozone.

On the trade front, while a 90-day pause in EU-US tariff retaliation offered temporary relief, the ECB remained concerned about the broader impact.

"Increased uncertainty is likely to reduce confidence among households and firms, and the adverse and volatile market response to the trade tensions is likely to have a tightening impact on financing conditions. These factors may further weigh on the economic outlook for the euro area," the press release further added. 

The euro (EUR:USD) -0.4% to $1.135.

ETFs to track: (EWG), (GF), (EWI), (EWQ), (FGM), (DAX)

data.title: Allstate estimates catastrophe losses to jump in March
data.description:
Property and casualty insurer Allstate (NYSE:ALL) announced on Thursday estimated catastrophe losses of $1.04B for March, surging from $92M in February.

March after-tax figure stood at $818M, compared to $73M in the prior month.

"Catastrophe losses for March include 11 events with approximately 80% of the losses related to four geographically widespread wind and hail events," Allstate said in a filing.

For Q1, the insurer estimates catastrophe losses of $2.20B, or $1.74B after-tax.

data.title: Atea Pharmaceuticals announces share repurchase program
data.description:
• Atea Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:AVIR) announced on Thursday the appointment of Howard H. Berman to its board. 
  • Atea also announced that its board has authorized, and will promptly begin executing upon, the repurchase of up to $25M of the company’s common stock.
  • Atea Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:AVIR) shares were up more than 3% in premarket trading.

data.title: LVMH Moët Hennessy raises CEO retirement age to give Arnault another 9 years
data.description:
LVMH Moët Hennessy (OTCPK:LVMHF) (OTCPK:LVMUY) shareholders approved a change to the company’s bylaws that would raise the maximum age of chairman and CEO to 85 from 80, giving Bernard Arnault control of the luxury conglomerate for another 9 years.

This is the second time the maximum age was raised in the last three years.

Arnault has run the company since the late 80s, when he and two other associates merged Christian Dior with Louis Vuitton and Moët Hennessy, creating the iconic LVMH global brand.  Over the years, strategic and profitable acquisitions that included Tiffany’s, Bulgari, Givenchy, Fendi, and Sephora vaulted LVMH (OTCPK:LVMHF) (OTCPK:LVMUY) to become Europe’s largest company and made Arnault one of the richest men in the world.

And while his two eldest children hold executive positions at the company, Arnault has not yet named a successor.

Although the stock has suffered lately from geopolitical events and sluggish demand out of China, the company remains the largest luxury brand in the world, and a bellwether to the state of the global economy. Its most recent quarterly results, which showed an unexpected drop op in sales, rattled investors and reverberated through the luxury category.

LVMH (OTCPK:LVMHF) (OTCPK:LVMUY) shares are fractionally lower on the Paris stock exchange.

data.title: American Express Q1 earnings beat as NII rises, credit provision drops
data.description:
American Express (NYSE:AXP) Q1 earnings and revenue on Thursday beat the average analyst estimate, as its net interest income and card fee revenue rose as its provision for credit losses declined.

Its network volume, though, declined more than expected from the prior quarter. American Express (NYSE:AXP) stock dipped 1.3% in premarket trading.

The credit card lender and network now maintained its 2025 guidance of $15.00-$15.50 ($15.26 consensus) and 8%-10% revenue growth. 

Q1 total network volume of $439.6B, trailing the Visible Alpha consensus of $443.3B, dropped from $464.0B in Q4 and advanced from $419.2B in Q1 2024. Network volume increased 5% Y/Y vs. +7% Y/Y in the prior quarter. Total billed business increased 6% Y/Y to $387.4B, and compared with 8% growth in Q4.

Q1 GAAP EPS of $3.64, beating the $3.47 consensus, increased from $3.04 in Q4 2024 and $3.33 in last year’s Q1.

Q1 revenue was $17.0B, topping the $16.9B consensus, slipped from $17.2B in the previous quarter and climbed from $15.8B a year ago.

"Our performance across key areas, including Card Member spending, customer retention, demand for our premium products, and credit performance, continued to be strong across our customer base, consistent with and in many cases better than what we saw in 2024," said Chairman and CEO Stephen J. Squeri.

Net card fee revenue grew to $2.33B from $2.25B in Q4 and $1.97B in last years’ Q1.

American Express (AXP) acquired 3.4M new cards during the quarter, compared with 3.0M new cards in the prior quarter and 3.4M new cards in Q1 2024.

Q1 net interest income of $4.17B, vs. the Visible Alpha consensus of $4.10B, climbed from $4.04B in the previous quarter and $3.77B a year ago.

Provision for credit losses of $1.15B, vs.the Visible Alpha estimate of $1.38B, decreased from $1.29B in Q4 and $1.27 B in last year’s Q1.

Consolidated expenses of $12.5B, less than the VA estimate of $12.4B, fell from $13.1B in the prior quarter and increased from $11.4B a year ago.

Card member loans decreased to $139,.2B from $139.7 in the prior quarter and increased from $126.6B a year ago. Net write-off (principal, interest, and fees) was %, vs. 2.2% in Q4 and % in Q1 2024.

Conference call at 8:30 AM ET.

Earlier, American Express GAAP EPS of $3.64 beats by $0.17, revenue of $16.97B beats by $40M

data.title: VersaBank adds second US RPP partner
data.description:
• VersaBank (NASDAQ:VBNK) said on Thursday that through its wholly owned subsidiary, VersaBank USA National Association, it has  entered into an agreement with its second US Receivable Purchase Program partner under which the partner will leverage VersaBank's RPP to fund a portion of its loan and lease originations.
  • VersaBank also announced its US RPP portfolio has surpassed US$70 million in assets in only 75 days since adding its first partner US RPP partner on January 30, 2025. The bank is on target to achieve US$290 million in US RPP in fiscal 2025.
  • VersaBank also announced that it has significantly expanded its nationwide network of authorized deposit brokers in Canada with the addition of Bank of Montreal Nesbitt Burns. The addition of BMO Nesbitt Burns to the bank's deposit broker network is expected to further contribute to expansion of VersaBank's NIM in fiscal 2025.
  • The bank further announced that it remains on track to reach its target of $1 billion of commitments in Canada Mortgage and Housing Corporation by the end of fiscal 2025. 
  • VersaBank (NASDAQ:VBNK) shares were up 2.75% in premarket trading.

data.title: Earnings snapshot: Charles Schwab beats Q1 top-line and bottom-line estimates
